2603.11625.title: MedPruner: Training-Free Hierarchical Token Pruning for Efficient 3D Medical Image Understanding in Vision-Language Models
2603.11625.paper_summary: While specialized Medical Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable success in interpreting 2D and 3D medical modalities, their deployment for 3D volumetric data remains constrained by significant computational inefficiencies. Current architectures typically suffer from massive anatomical redundancy due to the direct concatenation of consecutive 2D slices and lack the flexibility to handle heterogeneous information densities across different slices using fixed pruning ratios. To address these challenges, we propose MedPruner, a training-free and model-agnostic hierarchical token pruning framework specifically designed for efficient 3D medical image understanding. MedPruner introduces a two-stage mechanism: an Inter-slice Anchor-based Filtering module to eliminate slice-level temporal redundancy, followed by a Dynamic Information Nucleus Selection strategy that achieves adaptive token-level compression by quantifying cumulative attention weights. Extensive experiments on three 3D medical benchmarks and across three diverse medical VLMs reveal massive token redundancy in existing architectures. Notably, MedPruner enables models such as MedGemma to maintain or even exceed their original performance while retaining fewer than 5% of visual tokens, thereby drastically reducing computational overhead and validating the necessity of dynamic token selection for practical clinical deployment. Our code will be released.
2603.11625.paper_summary_zh: 專門的醫療視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在解釋 2D 和 3D 醫療模態方面取得了顯著成功，但其在 3D 體積數據的部署仍受到重大計算效率低下的限制。當前架構通常因為直接串接連續的 2D 切片而遭受大量解剖冗餘，並且缺乏靈活性來處理不同切片之間的異質信息密度，使用固定的修剪比率。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 MedPruner，一個無需訓練且與模型無關的分層標記修剪框架，專門設計用於高效的 3D 醫療影像理解。MedPruner 引入了一個兩階段機制：一個基於切片間錨點的過濾模組，用於消除切片級的時間冗餘，隨後是一個動態信息核選擇策略，通過量化累積注意力權重來實現自適應的標記級壓縮。在三個 3D 醫療基準和三個不同的醫療 VLM 上進行的廣泛實驗顯示，現有架構中存在大量標記冗餘。值得注意的是，MedPruner 使得像 MedGemma 這樣的模型能夠保持甚至超越其原始性能，同時保留少於 5% 的視覺標記，從而大幅降低計算開銷，並驗證了動態標記選擇在實際臨床部署中的必要性。我們的代碼將會發布。
2603.11625.author: Shengyuan Liu et.al.
2603.11625.authors: Shengyuan Liu, Zanting Ye, Yunrui Lin, Chen Hu, Wanting Geng, Xu Han, Bulat Ibragimov, Yefeng Zheng, Yixuan Yuan
